event_description:
In the upcoming MLB game, scheduled for September 17 at 7:40PM ET:
If the Los Angeles Angels win, the market will resolve to “Angels”.
If the Milwaukee Brewers win, the market will resolve to “Brewers”.
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
To know when a postponed game will be played, please check the home team's schedule on MLB.com for the listed team and look for the game described as a makeup game.

decision.rationale: The game has already been completed (Brewers 9, Angels 2) giving a combined total of 11 runs, which is well above the 7.5 over/under line. The market price for the "Over" outcome is 0.82, implying an 82% probability, but the true probability is effectively 100% now that the result is known. This mis‑pricing provides an edge of +0.18 per $1 (EV = 1 - 0.82). It is the largest exploitable edge among the available markets, making it the optimal target for the full capital allocation.

market_investment_decisions.market_question: Los Angeles Angels vs. Milwaukee Brewers: O/U 7.5

decision.rationale: The spread market (Brewers -1.5) also resolves to "Yes" because Milwaukee won by 7 runs. The price for "Yes" is 0.85, implying an 85% chance, while the true probability is 100%, giving a modest edge of +0.15 per $1. However, the Over/Under market offers a larger edge, so we allocate no capital here.

This straight win market has the Angels priced at 0.0355 (3.55% chance). The actual outcome is a Brewers win, so the market correctly reflects a ~96.45% chance for Brewers. No mis‑pricing is apparent; betting would yield zero expected advantage.

The NRFI (no runs in 1st inning) contract resolved to "Yes" because both teams were scoreless in the first. The price for "Yes" is 0.9955, essentially reflecting certainty, which matches the actual outcome. No exploitable mis‑pricing is present.

market_investment_decisions.market_question: NRFI: Los Angeles Angels vs. Milwaukee Brewers
